beats.narration_text: The part that stuck with me is that the .imscc cartridge is a file I own that keeps running even if you disappear tomorrow. I teach on Canvas and want to pressure-test that no-lock-in claim. Walk me through: (1) exactly what happens after I upload and confirm the cartridge — which modules, videos, and exams land where; (2) how a white-label workshop keeps my brand on the front while your instructors build the material; (3) what could actually break my course a year out if the rights truly travel with the file.

beats.narration_text: The format is dot I M S C C — Canvas's own import format. No integration project, no I T ticket queue, no vendor review to schedule. Upload, confirm, teach.

props.heading: The .imscc format
props.body:
Canvas's native import format.
No IT ticket. No integration project.
No vendor review.

Upload → Confirm → Teach.

beats.narration_text: If you want humans in the room, we deliver custom training and workshops ourselves — your team, your cases, our instructors.

props.heading: Custom training
props.body:
Live workshops delivered by Humanitarians AI.
Your team. Your cases. Our instructors.

We can run the room.

beats.narration_text: Or white-label it: we build the workshop, your experts deliver it, and your brand carries it. Your bench gets the material. Your clients see you.

props.heading: White-label option
props.body:
We build it.
Your experts deliver it.
Your brand on it.

Your bench gets the material. Your clients see you.

beats.narration_text: Before the next beat: predict the total lock-in here — the platform fee, the seat license, the annual renewal. Count it up. Take a second.

props.command: what is the total lock-in cost here?
props.runningText: platform fee + seat license + annual renewal…

beats.narration_text: Zero. The cartridge is a file you own. The zip is a folder you own. The rights travel with them. If we disappeared tomorrow, your course keeps running — which is exactly how course material should work.

props.heading: Lock-in: zero.
props.body:
The cartridge is a file you own.
The zip is a folder you own.
The rights travel with them.

If we disappeared tomorrow, your course keeps running.

beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. One page. A cartridge into Canvas, training if you want us, white-label if you want you — and files you own either way.

artifactLines: .imscc export → direct Canvas upload: modules, videos, exams standing in place.
Custom training and live workshops, delivered by Humanitarians AI.
White-label: we build it, your experts deliver it, your brand carries it.
Hiring us funds a 501(c)(3): fellows, mentorship, and free AI education.
Phase gate: files you own · no platform, no seats, no renewal · your course outlives us.

beats.narration_text: Your turn. The day the zip lands, paste this into Claude. Ten minutes later you are announcing week one.
